sglang.title: [diffusion] Add --offload-during-compile to fit max-autotune on tight-memory GPUs
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
max-autotune (the default DiT torch.compile mode) needs substantially more peak GPU memory during compilation than steady-state inference does, so a large DiT can OOM at compile-warmup time on a GPU that comfortably fits inference — leaving no way to serve it compiled on that hardware.
Co

sglang.title: Fix SWA eviction tombstoning the last leaf
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
With SGLANG_OPT_UNIFIED_CACHE_FREE_OUT_OF_WINDOW_SLOTS enabled, cache_unfinished_req eagerly frees out-of-window SWA slots before inserting. On the radix path this subtracted window + page_size, and SGLANG_OPT_SWA_EVICT_DROP_PAGE_MARGIN dropped the extra page down to just window. With bot

Part of the fused MoE gate/topk kernel consolidation (#26771). SGLang has many per-model gate/topk kernels (grouped_topk.cuh, AOT topk_sigmoid/topk_softmax, the CUDA-JIT moe_fused_gate, flashinfer fused_topk_deepseek, …). #25835 introduced one Triton moe_fused_gate router and made it the

sglang.title: Move deferred mamba cow and clear
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The deferred mamba COW and clear are mamba-pool operations but currently live on the linear attention backend. This moves them into ModelRunner._forward_raw, where the forward already owns the pool, so they no longer depend on the attention backend.

Stabilize the XPU LoRA Punica kernel CI by splitting test_punica_ops.py into three separate pytest invocations, rather than running the full suite in a single process. This avoids the test failures that occur when all tests are executed together, and removes the need for an ever‑growing list

Closes #38173
Algorithm
Based on the ICML 2025 oral paper:

Accelerating LLM Inference with Lossless Speculative Decoding Algorithms for Heteroge

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix pooled Whisper encoder sliding-window kernel size
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The causal Whisper encoder pools block_pool_size encoder tokens per KV block, so its SlidingWindowSpec.sliding_window is expressed in pooled units for the KV cache manager. The attention kernel, however, reads its window from the same spec field but runs on the block_pool_sizex-expanded (unpooled) s

vllm.title: [BugFix][MRV2] Ensure all req slots are accounted for when scheduling
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
MRV2 assumes that there can be at most max_num_seqs active requests, which corresponds to the maximum running queue length in the scheduler. However, there are two edge cases where MRV2 slots can still be occupied by requests not in the running queue:

Those waiting for streaming input (in WAITING_F

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Tool Parser] poolside_v1: accept tool calls without newline after function name
vllm.author: joerowell
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
poolside_v1's non-streaming extract_tool_calls uses a func_detail_regex that requires a literal newline between the function name and the <arg_key> block:
r"<tool_call>([^\n]*)\n(.*)</tool_call>"
That matches GLM-4's format, but some models emit the tool call with no newline after the name —

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Gemma4] Keep image bidirectional attention within the sliding window
vllm.author: lucianommartins
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Gemma4 vision models that use blockwise bidirectional image attention
(use_bidirectional_attention="vision" → 12B, 26B-A4B, 31B) silently fell back
to causal-only attention over image tokens whenever a single image's
soft-token span exceeded the text sliding window (e.g. ~1100 soft tokens at
